title: Tapping for Pride in Organised Money
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing shame about past money chaos and finding pride in where you are now
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 55 of a 12-week money program. The script works with the shame of having been disorganised with money, the hiding from statements and plans, and reframes toward a clear-eyed pride in how far things have come.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 55 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the memory of past money chaos is pulling at your current sense of capability, or when you want to consolidate the feeling of having got yourself sorted.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 55 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the lingering shame of past money disorganisation: the statements avoided, the plans that didn't stick, the sense of not being someone who manages money well. The reframe names where you actually are now.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once felt behind and messy with money, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I hid from statements and plans, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried I wasn't good with money, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've avoided facing the details.
Side of eye: I felt overwhelmed by paperwork.
Under eye: I didn't know where everything was.
Under nose: I felt embarrassed by the chaos.
Chin: I thought being disorganised meant I wasn't capable.
Collarbone: I've grown so much since then.
Under arm: I now see how far I've come.
Top of head: I feel proud of my money organisation.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I am proud of my clear financial systems.
Side of eye: I know where every pound lives.
Under eye: I keep money flowing in order and ease.
Under nose: I feel capable as a money manager.
Chin: I trust my planning and records.
Collarbone: I love the calm of organised finances.
Under arm: I celebrate the confidence this gives me.
Top of head: I feel proud and clear with money.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 55 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
